message:
Stage 0.5 (typed) D0.R escalated on round 0 (before cap) — the directed revise cannot fix the findings in place: lem:neyman-gap-identity (wrong_formula): this D0.5 finding is a statement-level defect, not just a proof-text or wiring issue. The node currently asserts V_nu(pi)-V_nu(p)=S^2(pi-p)^2/[pi(1-p)], but the correct identity has denominator pi(1-pi). Under the HARD LIMIT, I cannot change a theorem/lemma/proposition statement or conclusion in place, and leaving the false statement in the core would not honestly discharge the finding. This therefore requires an orchestrator/proto rewind. The other flagged items are proof-DAG/prose issues that are in-place fixable once this statement-level defect is resolved.

message:
Stage 0.5 (typed) D0.R escalated on round 2 (before cap) — the directed revise cannot fix the findings in place: oeq:closed-form-frontier (empty_band_attainment): blocking statement-level issue. The current node asserts unconditional finite-support attainment, but the reproduced proof only supports attainment when the regular band, equivalently the feasible moment set F, is nonempty; when F is empty there is no extremal law to attain the frontier. No honest in-place dependency/proof/definition/prose edit can make that existing statement true without restating the node to split the empty-band case or changing the statement-level convention for K in the proto/orchestrator. thm:global-log-rate (novelty_one_sided_surrogate) is a prose/positioning issue rather than a new-math blocker, but this run must escalate early because the frontier finding cannot be resolved within the allowed non-statement edit scope.

message:
Stage 0.5 (typed) D0.R escalated on round 0 (before cap) — the directed revise cannot fix the findings in place: lem:local-neighborhood-cumulative-risk/dropped_factor: the exact identity gives g_{nu^(u,h)}(pi)=H_{nu^(u,h)}[p_h(1-p_h)/(pi(1-pi))](pi-p_h)^2, so the current route only yields a lower bound at scale H_h p_h(1-p_h)(pi-p_h)^2=S_h^2(pi-p_h)^2, not a universal constant times H_nu(pi-p_h)^2 on all M_tan. Fixing this honestly would require genuinely new math proving a stronger regret-vs-estimation inequality, or an orchestrator-level statement change/new interior hypothesis to absorb the missing p_h(1-p_h) factor. thm:instance-local-minimax/unsupported_scale: the claimed c_0 (kappa_nu-epsilon) log T bound depends on that unsupported H_nu dot_pi_nu(u)^2 / J_nu(u) scale, so it cannot be repaired by an in-place core edit; it needs a proto/theorem rewind or new math at the stated scale. The missing_dep and comparator findings are subordinate core-editable items once this kernel scale issue is resolved, but emitting a partial edit here would be hollow.
Open findings: dropped_factor@lem:local-neighborhood-cumulative-risk, unsupported_scale@thm:instance-local-minimax, missing_dep@thm:instance-local-minimax, missing_closest_lower_bound_comparator@thm:instance-local-minimax.
